votes.reason: Without a frictionless public replication package, every controversial result—especially the GPT-5.4 ranking and hint-removal split—will be dismissed before the benchmark can establish credibility.

The next priority should be a structured validation of the hint-removal effect via broader prompt ablations and a standard hint-free diagnostic, because this is the benchmark’s main scientific claim and main credibility risk.

Hint-free results should be reported alongside hinted results as a standard diagnostic rather than replacing the hinted track outright, since this preserves continuity while directly measuring scaffolding sensitivity.

The biggest current risk is that prompt fragility enables the narrative that Moreau Arena measures prompt compliance rather than strategic reasoning, which would undermine the benchmark’s core value proposition.
Track B/Lab Mode is strategically promising but should remain secondary until Track A has a defensible replication story and validated measurement basis.
